concept: Generative photorealism creates risks around misinformation, identity theft, fraud, and consent, requiring ethical boundaries for creators.

exercises.prompt: What is the core idea of generative media that we learned about?
exercises.explain: AI models generate synthetic media (images, audio, video) from scratch based on patterns learned from real data.

options.text: Creating realistic media using AI models trained on patterns
Enhancing the brightness of a regular smartphone photo
Compressing large video files to save storage space

cards.body: Imagine a video of you saying things you never said, or a photo of a place you have never visited. As AI-generated media becomes indistinguishable from reality, how do we trust what we see online?
cards.title: Can We Believe Our Eyes?

cards.body: Synthetic media refers to images, videos, audio, or text generated or altered by AI. Because it can look and sound incredibly realistic, it raises major ethical concerns about consent, truth, and identity.
cards.title: What is Synthetic Media?

cards.body: Think of synthetic media like a professional voice impersonator. If they use their skill to entertain, it is harmless. But if they pretend to be your bank manager to steal your password, it becomes dangerous fraud.
cards.title: The Impersonator's Choice
Voice Cloning Scams
cards.body: A scammer uses a short voice clip of a child to generate a synthetic audio message begging their parents for emergency money. The parents, believing it is real, transfer funds immediately. This is a real-world risk of synthetic voice cloning.

cards.title: Is All Synthetic Media Evil?
cards.body: Myth: Synthetic media is only bad and should be completely banned. Fact: It has amazing creative uses, like translating movies into different languages using the actors' real voices, or helping disabled creators speak. The key is consent and clear labeling!

cards.title: Setting the Boundaries

left.text: Creating AI avatars for a video game with clear disclosure.

right.text: Generating a fake video of a politician to influence an election.

cards.body: Let's look at the difference between ethical and unethical uses of synthetic media.

options.text: As long as it looks cool, it is ethically fine to use.
Using someone's likeness without their consent is unethical.
Only government officials need to give consent.

exercises.explain: Consent is the key boundary. Using someone's face or voice without permission is a direct violation of their identity and rights.
exercises.prompt: What is the primary ethical rule regarding using someone's voice or face in synthetic media?

exercises.explain: Even with good intentions, generating highly realistic fake media without clear labels can accidentally spread misinformation and confuse people.
exercises.prompt: True or False: Synthetic media is always safe as long as the creator did not intend to hurt anyone.

exercises.explain: Consent means giving permission. Creating realistic content of real people without their consent leads to identity theft and harassment.
exercises.sentence: Using AI to generate a video of a real person without their [blank] violates their digital identity.
exercises.prompt: Using AI to generate a video of a real person without their ___ violates their digital identity.

options.text: Entering a password and receiving a text verification code
Using an AI-cloned voice clip to bypass voice-activated security
Scanning a physical fingerprint on a smartphone reader

exercises.explain: Using synthetic voice cloning to bypass a bank's biometric security system is a malicious exploit using AI generation. The others are standard security measures.
exercises.prompt: Identify which scenario represents a malicious threat enabled by generative synthetic media:

options.text: Encrypting the video file so no one can copy it
Clearly labeling the media as AI-generated
Only sharing the content on private group chats

exercises.explain: Disclosing that an image or video is AI-generated (via labels or watermarks) prevents public deception and builds trust. The next lesson will cover how we detect synthetic media!
exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a recommended best practice for creators when sharing synthetic media publicly?

title: Ethics of Synthetic Media
